NKJV.1: So it was, as the multitude pressed about Him to hear the word of God, that He stood by the Lake of Gennesaret,
NKJV.2: and saw two boats standing by the lake; but the fishermen had gone from them and were washing their nets.
NKJV.3: Then He got into one of the boats, which was Simon’s, and asked him to put out a little from the land. And He sat down and taught the multitudes from the boat.
NKJV.4: When He had stopped speaking, He said to Simon, “Launch out into the deep and let down your nets for a catch.”
NKJV.5: But Simon answered and said to Him, “Master, we have toiled all night and caught nothing; nevertheless at Your word I will let down the net.”
NKJV.6: And when they had done this, they caught a great number of fish, and their net was breaking.
NKJV.7: So they signaled to their partners in the other boat to come and help them. And they came and filled both the boats, so that they began to sink.
NKJV.8: When Simon Peter saw it, he fell down at Jesus’ knees, saying, “Depart from me, for I am a sinful man, O Lord!”
NKJV.9: For he and all who were with him were astonished at the catch of fish which they had taken;
NKJV.10: and so also were James and John, the sons of Zebedee, who were partners with Simon. And Jesus said to Simon, “Do not be afraid. From now on you will catch men.”
NKJV.11: So when they had brought their boats to land, they forsook all and followed Him.
NKJV.12: And it happened when He was in a certain city, that behold, a man who was full of leprosy saw Jesus; and he fell on his face and implored Him, saying, “Lord, if You are willing, You can make me clean.”
NKJV.13: Then He put out His hand and touched him, saying, “I am willing; be cleansed.” Immediately the leprosy left him.
NKJV.14: And He charged him to tell no one, “But go and show yourself to the priest, and make an offering for your cleansing, as a testimony to them,  just as Moses commanded.”
NKJV.15: However, the report went around concerning Him all the more; and great multitudes came together to hear, and to be healed by Him of their infirmities.
NKJV.16: So He Himself often withdrew into the wilderness and prayed.
NKJV.17: Now it happened on a certain day, as He was teaching, that there were Pharisees and teachers of the law sitting by, who had come out of every town of Galilee, Judea, and Jerusalem. And the power of the Lord was present to heal them.
NKJV.18: Then behold, men brought on a bed a man who was paralyzed, whom they sought to bring in and lay before Him.
NKJV.19: And when they could not find how they might bring him in, because of the crowd, they went up on the housetop and let him down with his bed through the tiling into the midst before Jesus.
NKJV.20: When He saw their faith, He said to him, “Man, your sins are forgiven you.”
NKJV.21: And the scribes and the Pharisees began to reason, saying, “Who is this who speaks blasphemies? Who can forgive sins but God alone?”
NKJV.22: But when Jesus perceived their thoughts, He answered and said to them, “Why are you reasoning in your hearts?
NKJV.23: Which is easier, to say, ‘Your sins are forgiven you,’ or to say, ‘Rise up and walk’?
NKJV.24: But that you may know that the Son of Man has power on earth to forgive sins”—He said to the man who was paralyzed, “I say to you, arise, take up your bed, and go to your house.”
NKJV.25: Immediately he rose up before them, took up what he had been lying on, and departed to his own house, glorifying God.
NKJV.26: And they were all amazed, and they glorified God and were filled with fear, saying, “We have seen strange things today!”
NKJV.27: After these things He went out and saw a tax collector named Levi, sitting at the tax office. And He said to him, “Follow Me.”
NKJV.28: So he left all, rose up, and followed Him.
NKJV.29: Then Levi gave Him a great feast in his own house. And there were a great number of tax collectors and others who sat down with them.
NKJV.30: And their scribes and the Pharisees complained against His disciples, saying, “Why do You eat and drink with tax collectors and sinners?”
NKJV.31: Jesus answered and said to them, “Those who are well have no need of a physician, but those who are sick.
NKJV.32: I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners, to repentance.”
NKJV.33: Then they said to Him, “Why do the disciples of John fast often and make prayers, and likewise those of the Pharisees, but Yours eat and drink?”
NKJV.34: And He said to them, “Can you make the friends of the bridegroom fast while the  bridegroom is with them?
NKJV.35: But the days will come when the bridegroom will be taken away from them; then they will fast in those days.”
NKJV.36: Then He spoke a parable to them: “No one puts a piece from a new garment on an old one; otherwise the new makes a tear, and also the piece that was taken out of the new does not match the old.
NKJV.37: And no one puts new wine into old wineskins; or else the new wine will burst the wineskins and be spilled, and the wineskins will be ruined.
NKJV.38: But new wine must be put into new wineskins, and both are preserved.
NKJV.39: And no one, having drunk old wine, immediately desires new; for he says, ‘The old is better.’ ”

ESV.5: And Simon answered, “Master, we toiled all night and took nothing! But at your word I will let down the nets.”
ESV.6: And when they had done this, they enclosed a large number of fish, and their nets were breaking.
ESV.7: They signaled to their partners in the other boat to come and help them. And they came and filled both the boats, so that they began to sink.
ESV.8: But when Simon Peter saw it, he fell down at Jesus' knees, saying, “Depart from me, for I am a sinful man, O Lord.”
ESV.9: For he and all who were with him were astonished at the catch of fish that they had taken,
ESV.10: and so also were James and John, sons of Zebedee, who were partners with Simon. And Jesus said to Simon, “Do not be afraid; from now on you will be catching men.”
ESV.11: And when they had brought their boats to land, they left everything and followed him.
ESV.12: While he was in one of the cities, there came a man full of leprosy.  And when he saw Jesus, he fell on his face and begged him, “Lord, if you will, you can make me clean.”
ESV.13: And Jesus  stretched out his hand and touched him, saying, “I will; be clean.” And immediately the leprosy left him.
ESV.14: And he charged him to tell no one, but “go and show yourself to the priest, and make an offering for your cleansing, as Moses commanded, for a proof to them.”
ESV.15: But now even more the report about him went abroad, and great crowds gathered to hear him and to be healed of their infirmities.
ESV.16: But he would withdraw to desolate places and pray.
ESV.17: On one of those days, as he was teaching, Pharisees and teachers of the law were sitting there, who had come from every village of Galilee and Judea and from Jerusalem. And the power of the Lord was with him to heal.
ESV.18: And behold, some men were bringing on a bed a man who was paralyzed, and they were seeking to bring him in and lay him before Jesus,
ESV.19: but finding no way to bring him in, because of the crowd, they went up on the roof and let him down with his bed through the tiles into the midst before Jesus.
ESV.20: And when he saw their faith, he said, “Man, your sins are forgiven you.”
ESV.21: And the scribes and the Pharisees began to question, saying, “Who is this who speaks blasphemies? Who can forgive sins but God alone?”
ESV.22: When Jesus perceived their thoughts, he answered them, “Why do you question in your hearts?
ESV.23: Which is easier, to say, ‘Your sins are forgiven you,’ or to say, ‘Rise and walk’?
ESV.24: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins” — he said to the man who was paralyzed — “I say to you, rise, pick up your bed and go home.”
ESV.25: And immediately he rose up before them and picked up what he had been lying on and went home, glorifying God.
ESV.26: And amazement seized them all, and they glorified God and were filled with awe, saying, “We have seen extraordinary things today.”
ESV.27: After this he went out and saw a tax collector named Levi, sitting at the tax booth. And he said to him, “Follow me.”
ESV.28: And leaving everything, he rose and followed him.
ESV.29: And Levi made him a great feast in his house, and there was a large company of tax collectors and others reclining at table with them.
ESV.30: And the Pharisees and their scribes grumbled at his disciples, saying, “Why do you eat and drink with tax collectors and sinners?”
ESV.31: And Jesus answered them, “Those who are well have no need of a physician, but those who are sick.
ESV.32: I have not come to call the righteous but sinners to repentance.”
ESV.33: And they said to him, “The disciples of John fast often and offer prayers, and so do the disciples of the Pharisees, but yours eat and drink.”
ESV.34: And Jesus said to them,  “Can you make wedding guests fast while the bridegroom is with them?
ESV.35: The days will come when the bridegroom is taken away from them, and then they will fast in those days.”
ESV.36: He also told them a parable: “No one tears a piece from a new garment and puts it on an old garment. If he does, he will tear the new, and the piece from the new will not match the old.
ESV.37: And no one puts new wine into old wineskins. If he does, the new wine will burst the skins and it will be spilled, and the skins will be destroyed.
ESV.38: But new wine must be put into fresh wineskins.
ESV.39: And no one after drinking old wine desires new, for he says, ‘The old is good.’”

NLT.1: One day as Jesus was preaching on the shore of the Sea of Galilee, great crowds pressed in on him to listen to the word of God.
NLT.2: He noticed two empty boats at the water’s edge, for the fishermen had left them and were washing their nets.
NLT.3: Stepping into one of the boats, Jesus asked Simon, its owner, to push it out into the water. So he sat in the boat and taught the crowds from there.
NLT.4: When he had finished speaking, he said to Simon, “Now go out where it is deeper, and let down your nets to catch some fish.”
NLT.5: “Master,” Simon replied, “we worked hard all last night and didn’t catch a thing. But if you say so, I’ll let the nets down again.”
NLT.6: And this time their nets were so full of fish they began to tear!
NLT.7: A shout for help brought their partners in the other boat, and soon both boats were filled with fish and on the verge of sinking.
NLT.8: When Simon Peter realized what had happened, he fell to his knees before Jesus and said, “Oh, Lord, please leave me — I’m such a sinful man.”
NLT.9: For he was awestruck by the number of fish they had caught, as were the others with him.
NLT.10: His partners, James and John, the sons of Zebedee, were also amazed. Jesus replied to Simon, “Don’t be afraid! From now on you’ll be fishing for people!”
NLT.11: And as soon as they landed, they left everything and followed Jesus.
NLT.12: In one of the villages, Jesus met a man with an advanced case of leprosy. When the man saw Jesus, he bowed with his face to the ground, begging to be healed. “Lord,” he said, “if you are willing, you can heal me and make me clean.”
NLT.13: Jesus reached out and touched him. “I am willing,” he said. “Be healed!” And instantly the leprosy disappeared.
NLT.14: Then Jesus instructed him not to tell anyone what had happened. He said, “Go to the priest and let him examine you. Take along the offering required in the law of Moses for those who have been healed of leprosy. This will be a public testimony that you have been cleansed.”
NLT.15: But despite Jesus’ instructions, the report of his power spread even faster, and vast crowds came to hear him preach and to be healed of their diseases.
NLT.16: But Jesus often withdrew to the wilderness for prayer.
NLT.17: One day while Jesus was teaching, some Pharisees and teachers of religious law were sitting nearby. (It seemed that these men showed up from every village in all Galilee and Judea, as well as from Jerusalem.) And the Lord’s healing power was strongly with Jesus.
NLT.18: Some men came carrying a paralyzed man on a sleeping mat. They tried to take him inside to Jesus,
NLT.19: but they couldn’t reach him because of the crowd. So they went up to the roof and took off some tiles. Then they lowered the sick man on his mat down into the crowd, right in front of Jesus.
NLT.20: Seeing their faith, Jesus said to the man, “Young man, your sins are forgiven.”
NLT.21: But the Pharisees and teachers of religious law said to themselves, “Who does he think he is? That’s blasphemy! Only God can forgive sins!”
NLT.22: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he asked them, “Why do you question this in your hearts?
NLT.23: Is it easier to say ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or ‘Stand up and walk’?
NLT.24: So I will prove to you that the Son of Man has the authority on earth to forgive sins.” Then Jesus turned to the paralyzed man and said, “Stand up, pick up your mat, and go home!”
NLT.25: And immediately, as everyone watched, the man jumped up, picked up his mat, and went home praising God.
NLT.26: Everyone was gripped with great wonder and awe, and they praised God, exclaiming, “We have seen amazing things today!”
NLT.27: Later, as Jesus left the town, he saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at his tax collector’s booth. “Follow me and be my disciple,” Jesus said to him.
NLT.28: So Levi got up, left everything, and followed him.
NLT.29: Later, Levi held a banquet in his home with Jesus as the guest of honor. Many of Levi’s fellow tax collectors and other guests also ate with them.
NLT.30: But the Pharisees and their teachers of religious law complained bitterly to Jesus’ disciples, “Why do you eat and drink with such scum? ”
NLT.31: Jesus answered them, “Healthy people don’t need a doctor — sick people do.
NLT.32: I have come to call not those who think they are righteous, but those who know they are sinners and need to repent.”
NLT.33: One day some people said to Jesus, “John the Baptist’s disciples fast and pray regularly, and so do the disciples of the Pharisees. Why are your disciples always eating and drinking?”
NLT.34: Jesus responded, “Do wedding guests fast while celebrating with the groom? Of course not.
NLT.35: But someday the groom will be taken away from them, and then they will fast.”
NLT.36: Then Jesus gave them this illustration: “No one tears a piece of cloth from a new garment and uses it to patch an old garment. For then the new garment would be ruined, and the new patch wouldn’t even match the old garment.
NLT.37: “And no one puts new wine into old wineskins. For the new wine would burst the wineskins, spilling the wine and ruining the skins.
NLT.38: New wine must be stored in new wineskins.
NLT.39: But no one who drinks the old wine seems to want the new wine. ‘The old is just fine,’ they say.”

WEB.1: Now while the multitude pressed on him and heard the word of God, he was standing by the lake of Gennesaret.
WEB.2: He saw two boats standing by the lake, but the fishermen had gone out of them, and were washing their nets.
WEB.3: He entered into one of the boats, which was Simon’s, and asked him to put out a little from the land. He sat down and taught the multitudes from the boat.
WEB.4: When he had finished speaking, he said to Simon, “Put out into the deep, and let down your nets for a catch.”
WEB.5: Simon answered him, “Master, we worked all night, and took nothing; but at your word I will let down the net.”
WEB.6: When they had done this, they caught a great multitude of fish, and their net was breaking.
WEB.7: They beckoned to their partners in the other boat, that they should come and help them. They came, and filled both boats, so that they began to sink.
WEB.8: But Simon Peter, when he saw it, fell down at Jesus’ knees, saying, “Depart from me, for I am a sinful man, Lord.”
WEB.9: For he was amazed, and all who were with him, at the catch of fish which they had caught;
WEB.10: and so also were James and John, sons of Zebedee, who were partners with Simon. Jesus said to Simon, “Don’t be afraid. From now on you will be catching people alive.”
WEB.11: When they had brought their boats to land, they left everything, and followed him.
WEB.12: While he was in one of the cities, behold, there was a man full of leprosy. When he saw Jesus, he fell on his face, and begged him, saying, “Lord, if you want to, you can make me clean.”
WEB.13: He stretched out his hand, and touched him, saying, “I want to. Be made clean.” Immediately the leprosy left him.
WEB.14: He commanded him to tell no one, “But go your way, and show yourself to the priest, and offer for your cleansing according to what Moses commanded, for a testimony to them.”
WEB.15: But the report concerning him spread much more, and great multitudes came together to hear, and to be healed by him of their infirmities.
WEB.16: But he withdrew himself into the desert, and prayed.
WEB.17: On one of those days, he was teaching; and there were Pharisees and teachers of the law sitting by, who had come out of every village of Galilee, Judea, and Jerusalem. The power of the Lord was with him to heal them.
WEB.18: Behold, men brought a paralyzed man on a cot, and they sought to bring him in to lay before Jesus.
WEB.19: Not finding a way to bring him in because of the multitude, they went up to the housetop, and let him down through the tiles with his cot into the middle before Jesus.
WEB.20: Seeing their faith, he said to him, “Man, your sins are forgiven you.”
WEB.21: The scribes and the Pharisees began to reason, saying, “Who is this that speaks blasphemies? Who can forgive sins, but God alone?”
WEB.22: But Jesus, perceiving their thoughts, answered them, “Why are you reasoning so in your hearts?
WEB.23: Which is easier to say, ‘Your sins are forgiven you;’ or to say, ‘Arise and walk?’
WEB.24: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins” (he said to the paralyzed man), “I tell you, arise, and take up your cot, and go to your house.”
WEB.25: Immediately he rose up before them, and took up that which he was laying on, and departed to his house, glorifying God.
WEB.26: Amazement took hold on all, and they glorified God. They were filled with fear, saying, “We have seen strange things today.”
WEB.27: After these things he went out, and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at the tax office, and said to him, “Follow me!”
WEB.28: He left everything, and rose up and followed him.
WEB.29: Levi made a great feast for him in his house. There was a great crowd of tax collectors and others who were reclining with them.
WEB.30: Their scribes and the Pharisees murmured against his disciples, saying, “Why do you eat and drink with the tax collectors and sinners?”
WEB.31: Jesus answered them, “Those who are healthy have no need for a physician, but those who are sick do.
WEB.32: I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.”
WEB.33: They said to him, “Why do John’s disciples often fast and pray, likewise also the disciples of the Pharisees, but yours eat and drink?”
WEB.34: He said to them, “Can you make the friends of the bridegroom fast, while the bridegroom is with them?
WEB.35: But the days will come when the bridegroom will be taken away from them. Then they will fast in those days.”
WEB.36: He also told a parable to them. “No one puts a piece from a new garment on an old garment, or else he will tear the new, and also the piece from the new will not match the old.
WEB.37: No one puts new wine into old wine skins, or else the new wine will burst the skins, and it will be spilled, and the skins will be destroyed.
WEB.38: But new wine must be put into fresh wine skins, and both are preserved.
WEB.39: No man having drunk old wine immediately desires new, for he says, ‘The old is better.’”

AMP.17: One day as He was teaching, there were Pharisees and teachers of the Law sitting there who had come from every village of Galilee and Judea and from Jerusalem. And the power of the Lord was present with Him to heal.
AMP.18: Some men came carrying on a stretcher a man who was paralyzed, and they tried to bring him in and lay him down in front of Jesus.
AMP.19: But finding no way to bring him in because of the crowd, they went up on the roof [and removed some tiles to make an opening] and lowered him through the tiles with his stretcher, into the middle of the crowd, in front of Jesus.
AMP.20: When Jesus saw their [active] faith [springing from confidence in Him], He said, "Man, your sins are forgiven."
AMP.21: The scribes and the Pharisees began to consider and question [the implications of what He had said], saying, "Who is this man who speaks blasphemies [by claiming the rights and prerogatives of God]? Who can forgive sins [that is, remove guilt, nullify sin's penalty, and assign righteousness] except God alone?"
AMP.22: But Jesus, knowing their [hostile] thoughts, answered them, "Why are you questioning [these things] in your hearts?
AMP.23: Which is easier, to say, 'Your sins are forgiven you,' or to say, 'Get up and walk'?
AMP.24: But, in order that you may know that the Son of Man (the Messiah) has authority and power on earth to forgive sins"—He said to the paralyzed man, "I say to you, get up, pick up your stretcher and go home."
AMP.25: He immediately stood up before them, picked up his stretcher, and went home glorifying and praising God.
AMP.26: They were all astonished, and they began glorifying God; and they were filled with [reverential] fear and kept saying, "We have seen wonderful and incredible things today!"
AMP.27: After this Jesus went out and noticed a tax collector named Levi (Matthew) sitting at the tax booth; and He said to him, "Follow Me [as My disciple, accepting Me as your Master and Teacher and walking the same path of life that I walk]."
AMP.28: And he left everything behind and got up and began to follow Jesus [as His disciple].
AMP.29: Levi (Matthew) gave a great banquet for Him at his house; and there was a large crowd of tax collectors and others who were reclining at the table with them.
AMP.30: The Pharisees and their scribes [seeing those with whom He was associating] began murmuring in discontent to His disciples, asking, "Why are you eating and drinking with the tax collectors and sinners [including non-observant Jews]?"
AMP.31: And Jesus replied to them, "It is not those who are healthy who need a physician, but [only] those who are sick.
AMP.32: I did not come to call the [self-proclaimed] righteous [who see no need to repent], but sinners to repentance [to change their old way of thinking, to turn from sin and to seek God and His righteousness]."
AMP.33: Then they said to Him, "The disciples of John [the Baptist] often practice fasting and offer prayers [of special petition], and so do the disciples of the Pharisees; but Yours eat and drink."
AMP.34: Jesus said to them, "Can you make the wedding guests of the bridegroom fast while he is with them?
AMP.35: But days [for mourning] will come when the bridegroom is [forcefully] taken away from them. They will fast in those days."
AMP.36: He also told them a parable: "No one tears a piece of cloth from a new garment and puts it on an old one; otherwise he will both tear the new, and the piece from the new will not match the old.
AMP.37: And no one puts new wine into old wineskins; otherwise the new [fermenting] wine will [expand and] burst the skins and it will be spilled out, and the skins will be ruined.
AMP.38: But new wine must be put into fresh wineskins.
AMP.39: And no one, after drinking old wine, wishes for new; for he says, 'The old is fine.'"

MSG.1: Once when he was standing on the shore of Lake Gennesaret, the crowd was pushing in on him to better hear the Word of God.
MSG.2: He noticed two boats tied up. The fishermen had just left them and were out scrubbing their nets.
MSG.3: He climbed into the boat that was Simon's and asked him to put out a little from the shore. Sitting there, using the boat for a pulpit, he taught the crowd.
MSG.4: When he finished teaching, he said to Simon, "Push out into deep water and let your nets out for a catch."
MSG.5: Simon said, "Master, we've been fishing hard all night and haven't caught even a minnow. But if you say so, I'll let out the nets."
MSG.6: It was no sooner said than done—a huge haul of fish, straining the nets past capacity.
MSG.7: They waved to their partners in the other boat to come help them. They filled both boats, nearly swamping them with the catch.
MSG.8: Simon Peter, when he saw it, fell to his knees before Jesus. "Master, leave. I'm a sinner and can't handle this holiness. Leave me to myself."
MSG.9: When they pulled in that catch of fish, awe overwhelmed Simon and everyone with him.
MSG.10: It was the same with James and John, Zebedee's sons, coworkers with Simon. Jesus said to Simon, "There is nothing to fear. From now on you'll be fishing for men and women."
MSG.11: They pulled their boats up on the beach, left them, nets and all, and followed him.
MSG.12: One day in one of the villages there was a man covered with leprosy. When he saw Jesus he fell down before him in prayer and said, "If you want to, you can cleanse me."
MSG.13: Jesus put out his hand, touched him, and said, "I want to. Be clean." Then and there his skin was smooth, the leprosy gone.
MSG.14: Jesus instructed him, "Don't talk about this all over town. Just quietly present your healed self to the priest, along with the offering ordered by Moses. Your cleansed and obedient life, not your words, will bear witness to what I have done."
MSG.15: But the man couldn't keep it to himself, and the word got out. Soon a large crowd of people had gathered to listen and be healed of their ailments.
MSG.16: As often as possible Jesus withdrew to out-of-the-way places for prayer.
MSG.17: One day as he was teaching, Pharisees and religion teachers were sitting around. They had come from nearly every village in Galilee and Judea, even as far away as Jerusalem, to be there. The healing power of God was on him.
MSG.18: Some men arrived carrying a paraplegic on a stretcher. They were looking for a way to get into the house and set him before Jesus.
MSG.19: When they couldn't find a way in because of the crowd, they went up on the roof, removed some tiles, and let him down in the middle of everyone, right in front of Jesus.
MSG.20: Impressed by their bold belief, he said, "Friend, I forgive your sins."
MSG.21: That set the religion scholars and Pharisees buzzing. "Who does he think he is? That's blasphemous talk! God and only God can forgive sins."
MSG.22: Jesus knew exactly what they were thinking and said, "Why all this gossipy whispering?
MSG.23: Which is simpler: to say 'I forgive your sins,' or to say 'Get up and start walking'?
MSG.24: Well, just so it's clear that I'm the Son of Man and authorized to do either, or both... ." He now spoke directly to the paraplegic: "Get up. Take your bedroll and go home."
MSG.25: Without a moment's hesitation, he did it—got up, took his blanket, and left for home, giving glory to God all the way.
MSG.26: The people rubbed their eyes, incredulous—and then also gave glory to God. Awestruck, they said, "We've never seen anything like that!"
MSG.27: After this he went out and saw a man named Levi at his work collecting taxes. Jesus said, "Come along with me."
MSG.28: And he did—walked away from everything and went with him.
MSG.29: Levi gave a large dinner at his home for Jesus. Everybody was there, tax men and other disreputable characters as guests at the dinner.
MSG.30: The Pharisees and their religion scholars came to his disciples greatly offended. "What is he doing eating and drinking with crooks and 'sinners'?"
MSG.31: Jesus heard about it and spoke up, "Who needs a doctor: the healthy or the sick?
MSG.32: I'm here inviting outsiders, not insiders—an invitation to a changed life, changed inside and out."
MSG.33: They asked him, "John's disciples are well-known for keeping fasts and saying prayers. Also the Pharisees. But you seem to spend most of your time at parties. Why?"
MSG.34: Jesus said, "When you're celebrating a wedding, you don't skimp on the cake and wine. You feast. Later you may need to pull in your belt, but this isn't the time. As long as the bride and groom are with you, you have a good time.
MSG.35: When the groom is gone, the fasting can begin. No one throws cold water on a friendly bonfire. This is Kingdom Come!
MSG.36: "No one cuts up a fine silk scarf to patch old work clothes; you want fabrics that match.
MSG.37: And you don't put wine in old, cracked bottles;
MSG.38: you get strong, clean bottles for your fresh vintage wine.
MSG.39: And no one who has ever tasted fine aged wine prefers unaged wine."

NIV.1:
The Calling of the First Disciples
One day as Jesus was standing by the Lake of Gennesaret, with the people crowding around him and listening to the word of God,
NIV.2: he saw at the water’s edge two boats, left there by the fishermen, who were washing their nets.
NIV.3: He got into one of the boats, the one belonging to Simon, and asked him to put out a little from shore. Then he sat down and taught the people from the boat.
NIV.4: When he had finished speaking, he said to Simon, “Put out into deep water, and let down the nets for a catch.”
NIV.5: Simon answered, “Master, we’ve worked hard all night and haven’t caught anything. But because you say so, I will let down the nets.”
NIV.6: When they had done so, they caught such a large number of fish that their nets began to break.
NIV.7: So they signaled their partners in the other boat to come and help them, and they came and filled both boats so full that they began to sink.
NIV.8: When Simon Peter saw this, he fell at Jesus’ knees and said, “Go away from me, Lord; I am a sinful man!”
NIV.9: For he and all his companions were astonished at the catch of fish they had taken,
NIV.10:
and so were James and John, the sons of Zebedee, Simon’s partners.
Then Jesus said to Simon, “Don’t be afraid; from now on you will catch men.”
NIV.11: So they pulled their boats up on shore, left everything and followed him.
NIV.12:
The Man With Leprosy
While Jesus was in one of the towns, a man came along who was covered with leprosy. When he saw Jesus, he fell with his face to the ground and begged him, “Lord, if you are willing, you can make me clean.”
NIV.13: Jesus reached out his hand and touched the man. “I am willing,” he said. “Be clean!” And immediately the leprosy left him.
NIV.14: Then Jesus ordered him, “Don’t tell anyone, but go, show yourself to the priest and offer the sacrifices that Moses commanded for your cleansing, as a testimony to them.”
NIV.15: Yet the news about him spread all the more, so that crowds of people came to hear him and to be healed of their sicknesses.
NIV.16: But Jesus often withdrew to lonely places and prayed.
NIV.17:
Jesus Heals a Paralytic
One day as he was teaching, Pharisees and teachers of the law, who had come from every village of Galilee and from Judea and Jerusalem, were sitting there. And the power of the Lord was present for him to heal the sick.
NIV.18: Some men came carrying a paralytic on a mat and tried to take him into the house to lay him before Jesus.
NIV.19: When they could not find a way to do this because of the crowd, they went up on the roof and lowered him on his mat through the tiles into the middle of the crowd, right in front of Jesus.
NIV.20: When Jesus saw their faith, he said, “Friend, your sins are forgiven.”
NIV.21: The Pharisees and the teachers of the law began thinking to themselves, “Who is this fellow who speaks blasphemy? Who can forgive sins but God alone?”
NIV.22: Jesus knew what they were thinking and asked, “Why are you thinking these things in your hearts?
NIV.23: Which is easier: to say, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Get up and walk’?
NIV.24: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins…” He said to the paralyzed man, “I tell you, get up, take your mat and go home.”
NIV.25: Immediately he stood up in front of them, took what he had been lying on and went home praising God.
NIV.26: Everyone was amazed and gave praise to God. They were filled with awe and said, “We have seen remarkable things today.”
NIV.27:
The Calling of Levi
After this, Jesus went out and saw a tax collector by the name of Levi sitting at his tax booth. “Follow me,” Jesus said to him,
NIV.28: and Levi got up, left everything and followed him.
NIV.29: Then Levi held a great banquet for Jesus at his house, and a large crowd of tax collectors and others were eating with them.
NIV.30: But the Pharisees and the teachers of the law who belonged to their sect complained to his disciples, “Why do you eat and drink with tax collectors and ‘sinners’?”
NIV.31: Jesus answered them, “It is not the healthy who need a doctor, but the sick.
NIV.32: I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.”
NIV.33:
Jesus Questioned About Fasting
They said to him, “John’s disciples often fast and pray, and so do the disciples of the Pharisees, but yours go on eating and drinking.”
NIV.34: Jesus answered, “Can you make the guests of the bridegroom fast while he is with them?
NIV.35: But the time will come when the bridegroom will be taken from them; in those days they will fast.”
NIV.36: He told them this parable: “No one tears a patch from a new garment and sews it on an old one. If he does, he will have torn the new garment, and the patch from the new will not match the old.
NIV.37: And no one pours new wine into old wineskins. If he does, the new wine will burst the skins, the wine will run out and the wineskins will be ruined.
NIV.38: No, new wine must be poured into new wineskins.
NIV.39: And no one after drinking old wine wants the new, for he says, ‘The old is better.’”
